subject: Anthropology
title: Slavery and Forced Labor
description: A 60-second lesson on Slavery and Forced Labor.

hook.text: The sugar in your tea once cost human lives. The global economy was built on forced labor, and its traces remain.

buildup.text: Slavery and forced labor have existed in many forms — from debt bondage to chattel slavery to modern trafficking.

discovery.text: The Atlantic slave trade fueled capitalism. Enslaved labor produced the wealth that built Europe and the Americas.

twist.text: Slavery didn't end — it transformed. Millions today work in forced labor, from factories to farms to domestic service.

climax.text: Forced labor reveals the dark side of economic growth. Prosperity for some always depends on exploitation of others.

punchline.text: Free markets were built on unfree labor.

quiz.question: What role did slavery play in global capitalism?
options: Enslaved labor produced the wealth that built Europe and the Americas
Slavery only existed in ancient times
